title: Fix hyperbolic unit test
body:
The `1x-T4-4-17` instance type were missing in the latest catalog ([commit](https://github.com/skypilot-org/skypilot-catalog/commit/06cb4087855609446f3ce9739d863ffd92cdbcd6)), causing this unit test to fail, as it's explicitly looking for that type.

This PR fixes it by switching to a valid instance type which exist on the latest catalog.

We probably should still investigate though why `1x-T4-4-17` went missing.

Tested (run the relevant ones):

- [x] Code formatting: install pre-commit (auto-check on commit) or `bash format.sh`
- [x] Any manual or new tests for this PR (please specify below)
- [ ] All smoke tests: `/smoke-test` (CI) or `pytest tests/test_smoke.py` (local)
- [x] Relevant individual tests: `/smoke-test -k test_name` (CI) or `pytest tests/test_smoke.py::test_name` (local)
- [ ] Backward compatibility: `/quicktest-core` (CI) or `pytest tests/smoke_tests/test_backward_compat.py` (local)

<!-- CI commands (/-prefixed) can only be triggered by repo members -->

step_plan.summary: Update the hyperbolic unit test to avoid depending on a hard-coded instance type removed from the catalog by specifying accelerators={'T4': 1} (no explicit instance_type), making the test resilient to catalog changes.

steps.operation: Change test to specify accelerators instead of a hard-coded instance_type
steps.reason: The test currently depends on a specific instance_type string ('1x-T4-4-17') that was removed from the external hyperbolic catalog. Constructing Resources with accelerators={'T4': 1} (and no instance_type) exercises Hyperbolic._get_feasible_launchable_resources() in a catalog-robust way: the implementation will resolve an appropriate instance type from the catalog at runtime, avoiding brittle hard-coded dependencies.
steps.side_effects: Only a unit test change (no production code modified). The test will now exercise the code path that resolves instance types from accelerators; if the catalog ever lacks any instance types for the given accelerator (T4), the test could still fail. Downstream tests or CI expectations that relied on the precise instance_type string will be unaffected because this change is localized to this test. Maintainers should run the targeted test (pytest tests/unit_tests/test_hyperbolic.py::test_hyperbolic_resource_feasibility) and the test suite to confirm behavior; add a clarifying comment in the test explaining why accelerators are used to avoid future brittleness.
